positioning: Fixed-price automation for UK ecommerce and wholesale teams, built by an operator who ran a 200,000 SKU multi-channel ecommerce operation.
primary_goal: Build authority for UK ecommerce and wholesale automation through high-quality, specific, operator-led articles. Quality beats quantity; avoid keyword spam and thin SEO pages.

search_console_plan.status: Submit this corrected sitemap in Google Search Console for the oliversconsulting.co.uk property.
search_console_plan.future_rule: Keep sitemap.xml, robots.txt and llms.txt updated after every article. Do not claim Google Search Console submission unless a real Search Console tool confirms it.

cadence_recommendation.quality_note: Two posts per week is the maximum recommended cadence, not a quota. Skip or refresh rather than publish weak content.

streams.name: Commercial SEO
streams.purpose: Capture problem-aware searches from ecommerce and wholesale teams looking to automate supplier files, reports, stock sync, listings and invoice admin.
example_topics: How to automate supplier price lists for Shopify and Amazon
What to automate before hiring another ecommerce administrator
How UK wholesalers can stop re-keying orders into Sage or Xero
Why your weekly sales report should not be built by hand

streams.name: Founder-led operator notes
streams.purpose: Build trust and authority using Henry Oliver's real experience running a 200,000 SKU ecommerce operation.
example_topics: What I learned running 200,000 SKUs across Shopify, Amazon and eBay
The hidden cost of fixing the spreadsheet every week
The difference between a software problem and a process problem
Why ROI maths should come before an automation build

topic_pillars.coverage: Supplier price lists, catalogue imports, bad-row checks, margin rules, file normalisation and supplier onboarding.
Monday packs, sales and margin reporting, stock cover, aged debtors, exception reports and channel reporting.
Shopify, Amazon, eBay, ERP data flow, listing updates, stock sync, channel drift and reconciliation.
Order-to-invoice work, PDFs, stockist portals, Sage, Xero, QuickBooks and re-keying between systems.
When admin hiring masks a process problem, how to calculate payback and when not to automate.
Practical views from Henry Oliver's 11 years running multi-channel ecommerce and 200,000 SKU operations.

quality_rules: Write for UK ecommerce and wholesale operators first, not search engines.
Quality beats quantity; two posts per week is a ceiling, not a quota.
No filler, generic automation hype or repeated points.
No keyword spam or pages that exist only to target a phrase.
Do not invent client results, statistics, dates, prices or case studies.
Every article must include a clear decision point, operational example or ROI calculation where relevant.
Each article must add authority through real workflow knowledge or founder/operator experience.
Avoid template-like card excerpts on the blog index.
Update sitemap.xml, robots.txt, llms.txt where useful and this content plan after every published article.
Record whether Search Console submission was unavailable or completed by a real tool.
